english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Adjuvantes autem exhortamur ne in vacuum gratiam Dei recipiatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνεργοῦντες δὲ καὶ παρακαλοῦμεν μὴ εἰς κενὸν τὴν χάριν τοῦ θεοῦ δέξασθαι ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: We then, *as* workers together *with him*, beseech *you* also that ye receive not the grace of God in vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait enim: Tempore accepto exaudivi te, et in die salutis adjuvi te. Ecce nunc tempus acceptabile, ecce nunc dies salutis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει γάρ καιρῷ δεκτῷ ἐπήκουσά σου καὶ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ σωτηρίας ἐβοήθησά σοι ἰδού, νῦν καιρὸς εὐπρόσδεκτος ἰδού, νῦν ἡμέρα σωτηρίας
verses.king_james: (For he saith, I have heard thee in a time accepted, and in the day of salvation have I succoured thee: behold, now *is* the accepted time; behold, now *is* the day of salvation.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemini dantes ullam offensionem, ut non vituperetur ministerium nostrum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδεμίαν ἐν μηδενὶ διδόντες προσκοπήν ἵνα μὴ μωμηθῇ ἡ διακονία
verses.king_james: Giving no offence in any thing, that the ministry be not blamed:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed in omnibus exhibeamus nosmetipsos sicut Dei ministros in multa patientia, in tribulationibus, in necessitatibus, in angustiis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἐν παντὶ συνιστώντες ἑαυτοὺς ὡς θεοῦ διάκονοι ἐν ὑπομονῇ πολλῇ ἐν θλίψεσιν ἐν ἀνάγκαις ἐν στενοχωρίαις
verses.king_james: But in all *things* approving ourselves as the ministers of God, in much patience, in afflictions, in necessities, in distresses,

verses.latin_vulgate: in plagis, in carceribus, in seditionibus, in laboribus, in vigiliis, in jejuniis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν πληγαῖς ἐν φυλακαῖς ἐν ἀκαταστασίαις ἐν κόποις ἐν ἀγρυπνίαις ἐν νηστείαις
verses.king_james: In stripes, in imprisonments, in tumults, in labours, in watchings, in fastings;

verses.latin_vulgate: in castitate, in scientia, in longanimitate, in suavitate, in Spiritu Sancto, in caritate non ficta,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἁγνότητι ἐν γνώσει ἐν μακροθυμίᾳ ἐν χρηστότητι ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ ἐν ἀγάπῃ ἀνυποκρίτῳ
verses.king_james: By pureness, by knowledge, by longsuffering, by kindness, by the Holy Ghost, by love unfeigned,

verses.latin_vulgate: in verbo veritatis, in virtute Dei, per arma justitiæ a dextris et a sinistris,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν λόγῳ ἀληθείας ἐν δυνάμει θεοῦ διὰ τῶν ὅπλων τῆς δικαιοσύνης τῶν δεξιῶν καὶ ἀριστερῶν
verses.king_james: By the word of truth, by the power of God, by the armour of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,

verses.latin_vulgate: per gloriam, et ignobilitatem, per infamiam, et bonam famam: ut seductores, et veraces, sicut qui ignoti, et cogniti:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ δόξης καὶ ἀτιμίας διὰ δυσφημίας καὶ εὐφημίας ὡς πλάνοι καὶ ἀληθεῖς
verses.king_james: By honour and dishonour, by evil report and good report: as deceivers, and *yet* true;

verses.latin_vulgate: quasi morientes, et ecce vivimus: ut castigati, et non mortificati:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς ἀγνοούμενοι καὶ ἐπιγινωσκόμενοι ὡς ἀποθνῄσκοντες καὶ ἰδού, ζῶμεν ὡς παιδευόμενοι καὶ μὴ θανατούμενοι
verses.king_james: As unknown, and *yet* well known; as dying, and, behold, we live; as chastened, and not killed;

verses.latin_vulgate: quasi tristes, semper autem gaudentes: sicut egentes, multos autem locupletantes: tamquam nihil habentes, et omnia possidentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς λυπούμενοι ἀεὶ δὲ χαίροντες ὡς πτωχοὶ πολλοὺς δὲ πλουτίζοντες ὡς μηδὲν ἔχοντες καὶ πάντα κατέχοντες
verses.king_james: As sorrowful, yet alway rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and *yet* possessing all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Os nostrum patet ad vos, o Corinthii; Corinthians nostrum dilatatum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ στόμα ἡμῶν ἀνέῳγεν πρὸς ὑμᾶς κορίνθιοι ἡ καρδία ἡμῶν πεπλάτυνται
verses.king_james: O *ye* Corinthians, our mouth is open unto you, our heart is enlarged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non angustiamini in nobis: angustiamini autem in visceribus vestris:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ στενοχωρεῖσθε ἐν ἡμῖν στενοχωρεῖσθε δὲ ἐν τοῖς σπλάγχνοις ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Ye are not straitened in us, but ye are straitened in your own bowels.

verses.latin_vulgate: eamdem autem habentes remunerationem, tamquam filiis dico, dilatamini et vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν δὲ αὐτὴν ἀντιμισθίαν ὡς τέκνοις λέγω πλατύνθητε καὶ ὑμεῖς
verses.king_james: Now for a recompence in the same, (I speak as unto *my* children,) be ye also enlarged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite jugum ducere cum infidelibus. Quæ enim participatio justitiæ cum iniquitate? aut quæ societas luci ad tenebras?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ γίνεσθε ἑτεροζυγοῦντες ἀπίστοις τίς γὰρ μετοχὴ δικαιοσύνῃ καὶ ἀνομίᾳ τίς δὲ κοινωνία φωτὶ πρὸς σκότος
verses.king_james: Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness? and what communion hath light with darkness?

verses.latin_vulgate: quæ autem conventio Christi ad Belial? aut quæ pars fideli cum infideli?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς δὲ συμφώνησις χριστῷ πρὸς βελιάρ ἢ τίς μερὶς πιστῷ μετὰ ἀπίστου
verses.king_james: And what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel?

verses.latin_vulgate: qui autem consensus templo Dei cum idolis? vos enim estis templum Dei vivi, sicut dicit Deus: [Quoniam inhabitabo in illis, et inambulabo inter eos, et ero illorum Deus, et ipsi erunt mihi populus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς δὲ συγκατάθεσις ναῷ θεοῦ μετὰ εἰδώλων ὑμεῖς γὰρ ναὸς θεοῦ ἐστε ζῶντος καθὼς εἶπεν ὁ θεὸς ὅτι ἐνοικήσω ἐν αὐτοῖς καὶ ἐμπεριπατήσω καὶ ἔσομαι αὐτῶν θεός καὶ αὐτοὶ ἔσονταί μοι λαός
verses.king_james: And what agreement hath the temple of God with idols? for ye are the temple of the living God; as God hath said, I will dwell in them, and walk in *them*; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod exite de medio eorum, et separamini, dicit Dominus, et immundum ne tetigeritis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ ἐξέλθετε ἐκ μέσου αὐτῶν καὶ ἀφορίσθητε λέγει κύριος καὶ ἀκαθάρτου μὴ ἅπτεσθε κἀγὼ εἰσδέξομαι ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: Wherefore come out from among them, and be ye separate, saith the Lord, and touch not the unclean *thing*; and I will receive you,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ego recipiam vos: et ero vobis in patrem, et vos eritis mihi in filios et filias, dicit Dominus omnipotens.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔσομαι ὑμῖν εἰς πατέρα καὶ ὑμεῖς ἔσεσθέ μοι εἰς υἱοὺς καὶ θυγατέρας λέγει κύριος παντοκράτωρ
verses.king_james: And will be a Father unto you, and ye shall be my sons and daughters, saith the Lord Almighty.
